label: A2 — Elementary
blurb: 5 partes, 25 preguntas: imágenes, completar, opción múltiple y emparejar.

audios.title: Part 1 · Questions 1–5
audios.intro: For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). You will hear five short conversations. Use the arrow ▶ or the numbers below to move to the next conversation.
scripts: Question one. Boy: Is it still raining, Mum? Mum: No, look out of the window — the snow has started! Boy: Great, let's make a snowman.
Question two. Girl: If it rains tomorrow, shall we go to the café? Boy: It's always full on Saturdays. Let's go to the library — they have a games afternoon. Girl: Perfect.
Question three. Mum: Take your umbrella, Jack — it's going to rain. Boy: I can't find it, so I'm taking Dad's big hat instead. Mum: Well, that's better than nothing.
Question four. Man: The radio says the rain will stop at four o'clock. Woman: Yesterday they said half past four, and it rained all day! Man: Let's hope they're right today.
Question five. Girl: Are we cycling to the match? Boy: In this weather? No way. Dad can't drive us, so we'll take the bus. Girl: OK, I'll check the times.

questions.q: Where will they go if it rains?
What will Jack take with him?
What time does the radio say the rain will stop today?
How will they get to the match?

audios.title: Part 2 · Questions 6–10
audios.intro: You will hear a teacher telling students about the class picnic. For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one word or a number or a date or a time.
scripts: Listen to a teacher talking about the class picnic. Teacher: Now, about our class picnic on Tuesday. The weather report says it will be sunny in the morning but windy later, so we will leave at ten o'clock, not at midday. We're walking to Green Park — it's only twenty minutes away. Everyone should bring water and a sun hat. And if it rains, we will stay at school and watch a film in room five.

questions.label: If it rains: film in room ____

audios.title: Part 3 · Questions 11–15
audios.intro: You will hear Daniel talking to his friend Kate about their weekend plans. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Daniel: Kate, are we still going to the lake on Saturday? Kate: I checked the weather — it's going to rain all day Saturday, but Sunday will be sunny. Daniel: Sunday it is, then. Should we cycle there? Kate: It's too far. My mum said she'll take us in the car. Daniel: Great. Shall I bring my football? Kate: Bring your camera instead — there are beautiful birds at the lake. Daniel: OK. What time do we leave? Kate: Mum wants to leave at nine, but let's say half past nine, because you're always late! Daniel: Very funny.

questions.q: When will Daniel and Kate go to the lake?
Why did they change the day?
How will they travel to the lake?

audios.title: Part 4 · Questions 16–20
audios.intro: You will hear a boy called Noah talking about a camping trip. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Noah: Last month my family went camping by the river. Dad checked the weather before we left, and it said sunny all weekend — but on the first night there was a huge storm! Water came into the tent and my sleeping bag got completely wet. Mum wanted to drive home, but Dad fixed the tent in the morning, and the rest of the weekend was lovely. The best moment was swimming in the river on Sunday. Next time we're going in July, when the weather is safer — and I'm taking two sleeping bags!

questions.q: What did the weather report say?
What happened on the first night?
What was the best moment of the trip?
What will Noah take next time?

audios.title: Part 5 · Questions 21–25
audios.intro: You will hear Elena talking about her friends' plans for the rainy weekend. What will each person do? For each question, choose the correct answer.
scripts: Elena: It's going to rain all weekend, so everyone has indoor plans. Sam is going to bake a cake with his dad — chocolate, I hope! Lucy wants to paint pictures for her grandma's birthday. Oliver is finally going to clean his room; his mum says he can't go out until it's done. Hannah is visiting the science museum with her cousins. And Marcus will read a book — he got three new ones for his birthday.
